figures.caption: Figure 1: Comparison of three KGQA paradigms.

Table 1: Comparison of GraphWalker with existing agentic KGQA methods across three key properties: Global KG (operating on global KGs rather than pre-extracted subgraphs), Autonomous Interaction (interacting autonomously rather than via pre-defined workflows), and RL Optimization (incorporating reinforcement learning to optimize reasoning policies).

Table 2: Performance comparison (in percentage) of KGQA methods on CWQ and WebQSP. The best and second-best results are shown in bold and underlined, respectively. † denotes models evaluated under the GraphWalker interaction framework with full global KG.
Table 12: Key parameters during the training process.

Figure 2: Overview of our proposed GraphWalker. (a) Data Construction: a multi-phase pipeline for GraphSynth-15k and outcome-based rejection sampling for GraphRoll-6k. (b) Two-Stage SFT: Stage 1 establishes a broad exploration prior, while Stage 2 instills reflection and error recovery. (c) RL Optimization: GRPO with a simple exact-match reward unlocks a higher performance ceiling enabled by the two-stage SFT prior.
Table 8: LLM-based Relation Reranking Ablation Study. The best and second-best results are shown in bold and underlined, respectively.
Figure 6: Impact of data scale on performance. Left: scaling GraphSynth data improves EM and Retrieval Rate. Right: scaling GraphRoll data improves EM and Recovery Rate.
